title: Insight and the selection of ideas.

assets.abstract: Perhaps it is no accident that insight moments accompany some of humanity's most important discoveries in science, medicine, and art. Here we propose that feelings of insight play a central role in (heuristically) selecting an idea from the stream of consciousness by capturing attention and eliciting a sense of intuitive confidence permitting fast action under uncertainty. The mechanisms underlying this Eureka heuristic are explained within an active inference framework. First, implicit restruct

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Predictive processing — free energy principle, Bayesian brain, active inference, neural causality.
